page: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7i.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core processing and memory capabilities. The system achieved a top-tier rating in the Passmark CPU score and CPU encryption tests, alongside a strong multi-core CPU performance rating in stress-ng. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only large block bandwidth of 228.17 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 95.44 GB/sec. Database and caching operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong rating in Passmark database operations and 98.08 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Additionally, the hardware-assisted matrix multiplication capabilities enable strong performance in LLM inference, processing 288.05 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.24xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound applications, leveraging DDR5 memory speeds and dedicated compute resources to maximize throughput per instance. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which represents a design tradeoff for storage-heavy workloads. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, data-intensive ETL processes, and CPU-based machine learning inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor with x86_64 architecture and a 3.2 GHz clock speed. It features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but includes no local storage or GPU hardware. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supports efficient CPU-based LLM inference. This configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 38 Gbps network baseline, ideal for large databases and in-memory applications.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors at 3.2 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and database benchmarks
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise applications
